project.creator: SonNT
project.notes: Feature-based integration workbook for UC-86 Admin Log Export. Server export generation cases are backed by a local Jest run on 24/03/2026. Controller-header behavior and browser download behavior are derived from implementation and remain Untested until executed end-to-end in a running environment.

changeLog.description: Initial integration workbook generated from the audit-log controller, service, admin UI, and verified export test execution.

features.name: Admin Audit Log Export
features.testRequirement: Validate admin export of filtered audit logs as JSON, CSV, and XLSX, plus HTTP download headers and the browser download flow from the admin audit workspace.

functions.name: Admin Log Export
functions.description: Admin exports filtered audit-log data from the audit workspace into downloadable files.
functions.preCondition: Authenticated admin is authorized by JwtAuthGuard and RolesGuard and audit data exists for the selected filters.

testCases.description: Generate a JSON export payload that keeps metadata and filtered entity identifiers.
testCases.procedure: 1. Execute `cmd /c npm test -- audit-logs.service.spec.ts --runInBand` in `server`.\n2. Run the `exports JSON payloads with metadata` spec.\n3. Inspect the returned buffer, file name, and content type assertions.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Export content type is `application/json`.\n2. File name matches `audit-logs-*.json`.\n3. Serialized payload includes the filtered `entityId` value.
testCases.actualResults: Verified by local Jest execution on 24/03/2026. The spec passed and confirmed JSON content type, generated file name pattern, and presence of `\"entityId\": \"dispute-1\"` in the payload.
testCases.dependencies: Server dependencies installed and Jest runnable from `server`.
testCases.preConditions: AuditLogsService spec fixture provides at least one audit-log entity.

testCases.description: Generate a CSV export payload that flattens audit-log rows and preserves entity identifiers.
testCases.procedure: 1. Execute `cmd /c npm test -- audit-logs.service.spec.ts --runInBand` in `server`.\n2. Run the `exports CSV payloads with dispute entity ids` spec.\n3. Inspect the CSV assertions.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Export content type is `text/csv`.\n2. CSV header row includes `entityId`.\n3. CSV body includes the filtered dispute id.
testCases.actualResults: Verified by local Jest execution on 24/03/2026. The spec passed and confirmed CSV content type plus presence of the `entityId` header and `dispute-2` row value.
testCases.dependencies: Server dependencies installed and Jest runnable from `server`.
testCases.preConditions: AuditLogsService spec fixture provides at least one audit-log entity.

testCases.description: Generate an XLSX workbook export for audit analytics downloads.
testCases.procedure: 1. Execute `cmd /c npm test -- audit-logs.service.spec.ts --runInBand` in `server`.\n2. Run the `exports XLSX workbooks for audit analytics exports` spec.\n3. Inspect the workbook buffer assertions.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. Export content type is the XLSX MIME type.\n2. File name matches `audit-logs-*.xlsx`.\n3. Workbook buffer starts as a valid ZIP/XLSX payload.
testCases.actualResults: Verified by local Jest execution on 24/03/2026. The spec passed and confirmed the XLSX MIME type, dynamic file name, and `PK` workbook signature.
testCases.dependencies: Server dependencies installed and Jest runnable from `server`.
testCases.preConditions: AuditLogsService spec fixture provides at least one audit-log entity.

testCases.description: Controller export endpoint applies response headers and records an EXPORT audit event for the admin action.
testCases.procedure: 1. Authenticate as an admin.\n2. Call `GET /audit-logs/export` with export filters and a selected format.\n3. Inspect response headers and the follow-up audit-log record.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. `Content-Type` matches the exported format.\n2. `Content-Disposition` contains the generated file name.\n3. The service logs an `EXPORT` event with filter metadata.
testCases.dependencies: Running backend with seeded audit-log data and an authenticated admin token.
testCases.preConditions: JwtAuthGuard and RolesGuard allow the request for an ADMIN user.
testCases.note: Code-derived from `audit-logs.controller.ts`; no dedicated endpoint execution evidence captured yet.

testCases.description: Admin audit workspace opens the export dialog and offers CSV and Excel export actions.
testCases.procedure:
1. Sign in as an admin.
2. Open `/admin/audit-logs`.
3. Click the `Export` button.
4. Inspect the dialog actions.
testCases.expectedResults: 1. The export dialog opens.\n2. CSV and Excel workbook actions are available.\n3. Buttons are disabled only while an export request is in progress.
testCases.dependencies: Running frontend connected to a reachable backend export endpoint.
testCases.preConditions: Admin can access the audit-log page and existing summary/table data loads successfully.
testCases.note: Code-derived from `AuditLogPage.tsx` dialog state and button wiring.

testCases.description: Successful export downloads the blob with the server-supplied filename and emits breadcrumb metadata with the selected format.
testCases.procedure: 1. Open the audit export dialog as an admin.\n2. Trigger CSV or XLSX export after setting filters.\n3. Observe the browser download name and emitted breadcrumb payload.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Client reads `content-disposition` from the response.
2. Browser download uses the server-provided file name or the format fallback.
3. A client breadcrumb named `audit-log-export` is sent with the chosen format.
4. The dialog closes after success.
testCases.dependencies: Running frontend, reachable backend, and browser download permissions enabled.
testCases.preConditions: Export request returns a blob response and response headers are exposed to the browser.
